summary: Backend-only testing for Harsha 3D portfolio FastAPI. All 9 pytest cases passed against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL covering /api/, /api/health, /api/profile, /api/projects, and full /api/contact lifecycle (valid POST + persistence, 422 invalid email, 422 empty name, honeypot silently-accepted-but-not-persisted, newest-first sorting on GET).

minor.issue: No pagination beyond `limit` query param and no auth/protection — contact submissions are publicly listable. Not a functional bug but a privacy/security smell for a portfolio's contact inbox.
Honeypot branch returns a fresh ContactMessage with a real UUID and timestamp to the bot, which could leak signal. Consider returning a generic 200 ack instead. Functional behavior matches spec.

action_items: Consider protecting GET /api/contact (admin-only) — currently anyone can list submissions.
Optional: honeypot branch should not return a generated id/created_at to avoid signaling bots.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py stores created_at as ISO string then re-parses on read — acceptable but inconsistent with using a native BSON datetime; mixed storage formats may cause sort issues if any legacy doc uses datetime instead of ISO string.
MongoDB _id is correctly excluded via projection in list_contacts — good.
EmailStr validation working (422 on invalid email).
Pydantic min_length=1 enforced for name/message — good.
No rate limiting on POST /api/contact — open to spam beyond honeypot.
CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers will block credentialed requests; tighten origins for prod.
seed_data_creation: Test inserts contact_messages with TEST_ prefix (not cleaned up — collection is fresh test_database).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio_api.py covers all 9 listed cases and all pass. Frontend testing was explicitly skipped per request. Re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio_api.py -v
